title: Exact Entanglement Dynamics Beyond Nearest-Neighbor Dual-Unitary Floquet Systems
abstract: Exact results using dual-unitarity largely rely on nearest-neighbor structures, while finite-range interactions typically lead to complications. Going beyond the usual nearest-neighbor setting, we introduce an analytically tractable family of finite-range kicked Ising models that admit exact closed-form entanglement dynamics. The construction is based on a staggered structure in which dual-unitarity is present on sublattices that are then coupled to each other. The central observation is that these inter-sublattice couplings do not obstruct the dual-unitarity of the resulting model. For the minimal interaction range of $r= 2$, we derive exact expressions for all the $n-$Rényi entanglement entropies at all times and show that the result is the sum of the two coupled sublattice contributions. Our framework extends naturally to larger finite interaction ranges and to systems with heterogeneous local Hilbert spaces, without additional assumptions. It thus provides a controlled setting for studying exact entanglement growth beyond strictly nearest-neighbor dual-unitary models.
summary: This paper develops an exact analytical method to calculate entanglement dynamics in Floquet spin models with finite-range interactions, moving beyond the limitations of nearest-neighbor dual-unitary systems. By constructing a staggered sublattice structure that preserves duality, the authors derive explicit formulas for $n$-Rényi entropies. This work provides deep theoretical insight into how interaction range controls entanglement growth rates in quantum many-body physics.
main_problem: The research aims to extend the study of exactly solvable entanglement dynamics beyond nearest-neighbor dual-unitary Floquet systems to incorporate finite-range interactions.
main_result: They derive exact closed-form expressions for $n$-Rényi entanglement entropies, showing that the interaction range acts as a tunable multiplier on the entanglement growth rate.
method: The analysis utilizes space-time duality and the replica trick to evaluate moments of the reduced density matrix ($	ext{tr}[(ho_X(t))^n]$) for structured spin chains under Floquet evolution.
model_or_system: The system is a spin-1/2 chain governed by a Floquet unitary operator incorporating range-$r$ interactions, which are analytically tractable by structuring the lattice into coupled sublattices that maintain dual-unitarity.
key_observables: $n$-Rényi entanglement entropies ($S_\alpha^X(t)$), spectral properties (level spacing distribution, spectral form factor).
important_parameters: Interaction range $r$, time $t$, and the replica parameter $\alpha$ (or $n$).
main_assumptions: The construction relies on grouping sites into enlarged unit cells to preserve dual-unitarity despite inter-sublattice couplings, and specific solvable initial states are analyzed.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate schematic constructions of the $r=2$ model (showing coupled sublattices) and numerical plots showing the time evolution of Rényi entropy for various initial conditions.
paper_structure: The paper develops the formalism by defining the Hamiltonian structure, applying space-time duality to find exact formulas for density matrix moments using the replica trick, and finally deriving explicit expressions for entanglement entropy in terms of these calculated traces.
why_interesting: This work provides a controlled theoretical framework to study how increasing interaction range affects quantum information measures like entanglement growth rates in periodically driven many-body systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic of the range r = 2 construction. Black links form two self-dual KFIM sublattices, while gray links are arbitrary diagonal inter-sublattice couplings. These couplings make the Floquet dynamics genuinely coupled, yet they do not spoil dual-unitarity.

FIG. 2. The R´enyi-2 entropy for range-2 model evolving from various initial states. We choose total spins L = 30 and subsystem size to be N = 13. The initial state is of the form defined by Eq. (7) and θk = θ, ϕk = ϕ.

FIG. 3. The R´enyi-2 entropy for a range-2 model from vari- ous initial states, in the thermodynamic limit obtained. θk, ϕk with k ∈{A, B} denotes the uniform value of θk, ϕk chosen for initial states in each sublattice. Inset shows instantaneous slope, ∆S(2) X (t−1/2)

FIG. 1. Schematic of the range r = 2 construction. Black links form two self-dual KFIM sublattices, while gray links are arbitrary diagonal inter-sublattice couplings. These couplings make the Floquet dynamics genuinely coupled, yet they do not spoil dual-unitarity in the enlarged unit cell.

FIG. 2. (a) Level spacing distribution of the (unfolded) quasi energies of the unitary operator, with L = 12, given by Eq.(10) for generic Jn, hn . We choose hn = 0.5 and Jn is chosen randomly from the interval [0, 1] and we average over 1024 realizations. The dashed curve corresponds to the Wigner surmise for circular orthogonal ensemble (COE). (b) Spectral form factor, as defined using Eq. (13) for the range-2 model. The result shows a clear ramp in agreement with COE results, shown as black dashed line.
